purpose: Freeze the approved launch business, billing, provider-procurement, verification, and evidence boundaries without claiming unfinished slides are delivered.
machineBoundary: Four product owners, customer products, monthly settlement, prepaid Tencent procurement, reusable verification, and per-slide completion evidence.

lifecycle.removalCondition: Replace only with a newer approved launch freeze and matching contract tests.

architectureAuthority.rule: The upstream repository owns target product boundaries; this contract owns the current implementation choices and delivery gaps.

owns: entitlements, renewal, expiry, and operator review
resource claim, attachment, and Workspace runtime
reservation, fulfillment, capture, release, renewal, and verification receipts
API keys, model routing, and request usage

monthlySettlement.captureRule: Capture exactly once only after Fabric reads back every required provider resource ID, PREPAID charge type, billing period, and ownership tag.
monthlySettlement.releaseRule: Release only after Fabric proves that no billable provider resource exists or that cancellation and provider refund completed.
monthlySettlement.retryRule: Reserve, provider mutation, claim, capture, and release each use stable operation-scoped idempotency identities.
monthlySettlement.currentImplementation: Direct negative Sub2API adjustment after Fabric prepare; hold, capture, and release APIs are not integrated.

providerProcurement.capacityCheck: Read-only prepaid inventory and quota check before reservation; it must not create a resource.
providerProcurement.customerProductRule: Provider SKU may vary by approved environment, but it must satisfy the customer product CPU and memory contract.
providerProcurement.currentImplementation: Fabric currently requires POSTPAID_BY_HOUR CVM pools and does not prove prepaid CBS.

perRunCleanup: test Workspace Deployment, Service, and Secret
test-only data under the verification path
isolated Control Plane, Fabric, and Ledger rows
verification.providerAcceptance: Explicit manual run only when the slot is first purchased, renewed, or prepaid procurement code changes.
verification.productionSmoke: Read-only service and existing-resource checks; no monthly charge and no provider mutation.
verification.currentImplementation: The production verifier spends real monthly balance and creates then deletes provider resources; it is forbidden for launch verification.

slides.business: A mapped customer signs in and sees the approved Basic and Pro service-plan definitions without exposing an internal verification SKU.
slides.currentState: Basic login and mapping are implemented; Pro was removed from the active catalog and requires restoration and evidence.

slides.business: The customer sees the live Sub2API wallet and an exact monthly resource quote before any reservation or provider mutation.
slides.currentState: Live balance, unavailable-state degradation, Basic quote, and integer pricing exist; Pro quote is not active.

slides.business: The monthly amount is atomically reserved in Sub2API so concurrent Gateway usage cannot spend it while resources open.
slides.currentState: Missing: OPL Cloud integrates only direct balance adjustment; no generic Sub2API reserve/capture/release client exists.
completionEvidence: live test-account hold and release readback

slides.business: Fabric opens one-month prepaid CVM and CBS resources after reservation without exposing provider billing details as customer products.
slides.currentState: Blocked: CVM creation, capacity checks, and tests require POSTPAID_BY_HOUR; prepaid CBS is unproven.
completionEvidence: no duplicate provider purchase under replay

slides.business: The reserved amount settles only after all prepaid resources are identified and assigned to the customer purchase.
slides.currentState: Missing: direct adjustment activates entitlement without a provider claim and capture protocol.

slides.business: The customer opens the assigned Workspace only after active entitlement and real runtime readiness, then authenticates to the workbench.
slides.currentState: Runtime readiness gating and password access are implemented; production authenticated browser and WebSocket evidence remain required.

slides.business: The Workspace uses a dedicated Sub2API test or customer key for model requests while Sub2API remains usage and key truth.
slides.currentState: Sub2API balance and external portal are connected; a Workspace model request and attributed usage readback are not proven.
completionEvidence: no key in Console or Ledger evidence

slides.business: Customer renewal reserves funds before manual Tencent renewal; failure never double-charges, double-renews, or silently releases an ambiguous reservation.
slides.currentState: Customer entitlement renewal and expiry exist; provider prepaid renewal and reservation settlement do not.

slides.business: Every release proves the real Workspace and Gateway path without buying or deleting monthly CVM/CBS resources per run.
slides.currentState: Blocked: the current paid verifier charges a real monthly product and creates then destroys resources on every run.
completionEvidence: stable provider IDs before and after
no Tencent purchase or deletion in run log

slides.business: A release is declared ready only from merged code, immutable images, runtime checks, recovery evidence, and a passing reusable verification run.
slides.currentState: CI and rollout workflows exist; production readiness checks, backup recovery, CLB/WebSocket, prepaid quota, and safe verification evidence are incomplete.
